About.AboutText:
Lucas Witthauer
(360)-241-4852 | witthauerl@spu.edu | Seattle,WA www.linkedin.com/in/lucaswitthauer/

Skills and Qualifications

●	Proficient in programming languages such as C++, Java, Assembly, Python, and JavaScript
●	Familiar with Software/technologies including Microsoft Suite, Arduino IDE, LTspice
●	Ability to learn on the fly
●	Concise communication skills

Education
Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering	Estimated Graduation June 2027
Seattle Pacific University, WA
●	GPA: 3.36
●	Recipient of Andy Grove Scholarship For Intel Employees’ Children
●	Relevant Courses: Calculus 1-3, LinAlg, Diff EQ, Physics 1-3, Object-Oriented Programming, Data Structures, Computer Login/Design, Circuits I-II, Computer Organization/Assembly Language, Electromagnetism, Electronics, Microcontrollers


Relevant Experiences
	
IR Frequency Detector(Circuit)                                                                                                                                      March 2026

I created a filter for a 10khz IR beam that requires 3 Bandpass Sallen-Key stages with Voltage Dividers on each. The output of the 3rd filter connects to a peak detector, which is then fed into an LM339 to control the logic behind detecting the 10khz signal regardless of other inputs, ultimately powering a single LED.

Traffic Light(Digital Logic)                                                                                                                                        December 2025 

I designed a traffic light using a bottom-up approach, beginning with the logic equations/gates to control the flow of a three-way intersection, then using these equations to build a physical circuit that powers 9 different LEDs based on the simulated queue of cars.

Local LLM Server(AI/MLt)	March 2026 - Present

I recently began working on creating a Local LLM Server with a Raspberry Pi 5. I will share more details as the project develops!



Additional Experiences
NCAA D2 II Soccer	August 2023 - Present
Western Washington University – Seattle Pacific University
●	Competing as an NCAA Division II student-athlete on the Seattle Pacific University Men’s Soccer Team, balancing 20+ hours per week of training, travel, and competition with full-time coursework.
●	Demonstrating commitment, resilience, and time management by maintaining academic performance while competing at a collegiate level.
●	Maintaining a team first mindset + Individual work ethic

Front Desk                                                                                                                                                        July 2025 - Present
Rose City Futsal - Portland, OR
●	Managed player check-ins, registrations, and scheduling for leagues, camps, and field rentals. Exposed to product development and engineering consulting in the sustainability industry
●	Utilized Excel, EZFacility, and PoS software to manage/track payments
●	Responded efficiently to customer inquiries, resolving scheduling conflicts and assisting with facility coordination.
